data.title: Adial Pharmaceuticals files to register resale of 6.7M shares from warrant conversions
data.description:
• Adial Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ADIL) is registering up to 6.73M shares of common stock for resale by certain selling stockholders.
  • The shares include over 6.5M issuable from Series B-1 and C-1 warrant exercises and about 223,000 from "Tail Fee Warrants" issued to H.C. Wainwright designees.
  • Adial will not receive proceeds from resale of the shares but will benefit from any cash warrant exercises.
  • ADIL shares up 3.2% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: VSee Health faces Nasdaq non-compliance over delayed filings
data.description:
• VSee Health (NASDAQ:VSEE) received a Nasdaq notice of non-compliance for failing to file its quarterly and annual reports.
  • VSee has until June 24, 2025, to submit a compliance plan to Nasdaq.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq may extend the deadline to regain compliance until October 13, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: TechTarget receives Nasdaq notice for delayed financial filings
data.description:
• TechTarget (NASDAQ:TTGT) was notified by Nasdaq on May 27, 2025, for failing to file its quarterly and annual reports.
  • The company is not in compliance with Nasdaq. TechTarget has until June 16, 2025, to submit a compliance plan to Nasdaq.
  • If the plan is accepted, the company may have until October 13, 2025, to file the delayed reports and regain compliance.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Motorsport Games files to sell 2.27M shares of Class A common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Motorsport Games (NASDAQ:MSGM) filed a prospectus to resale 2.27M shares of Class A common stock.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • Filing

data.title: Tronox upgraded at J.P. Morgan on improving titanium dioxide fundamentals
data.description:
Tronox (NYSE:TROX) +11.9% in Friday's trading after J.P. Morgan upgraded the chemical company to Overweight from Neutral with a $7 price target, raised from $5, saying titanium dioxide fundamentals are improving, and prices likely will move higher in Q2 or Q3 after a two-year period of decline.

JPM's Jeffrey Zekauskas noted the value of commodity companies often increases due to a combination of rising commodity prices, improved supply/demand balance, and a shift in macro sentiment, and these elements appear to be aligning favorably for Tronox (NYSE:TROX), the analyst said.

Zekauskas pointed to potential volume opportunities for Tronox (TROX), especially since ~400K tons/year of product have been shipped by China into the Indian and Brazilian markets, where tariffs topping $500/ton have been partly or fully implemented.

While Tronox (TROX) "generates minimal free cash flow after capital expenditures," Zekauskas said he believes the company "will cover its dividend in 2026 and 2027 as a base case."

Tronox (TROX) maintains a notable dividend yield of 9.8% and has consistently paid dividends for 14 consecutive years, despite current market challenges, the analyst said.

data.title: Wall Street set for a massive May advance as markets recover from tariff-driven swoon
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday was on track to post a strong monthly advance, though it was set for a mixed finish on the final day of May after U.S. President Donald Trump said China had "totally violated" a trade truce. 

An in-line reading of a key inflation gauge and an improvement in consumer sentiment were not enough to lift the mood.

Into the final half hour of trading, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was flat, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%. On a monthly basis, the S&P was up more than 6%.  

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 1 basis point at 4.43%, and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points at 3.93%.

The U.S. and China at the start of the month entered into a surprise trade agreement, under which they agreed to decrease tariff rates against each other for 90 days. But Trump on Friday said that China had violated that agreement. 

The President's accusation comes a day after legal back-and-forth over his "Liberation Day" tariffs. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit on Thursday issued an administrative stay blocking a ruling from a lower court on Wednesday that struck down the reciprocal tariffs imposed in April.  

“We expect the markets to remain headline-driven, especially headlines related to tariffs, of which there are many. As we start to see more clarity on trade, we expect more traditional drivers of markets, like earnings and fundamentals, to lead the way and drive sentiment and the trajectory of stocks,” said Clark Bellin, president and CIO at Bellwether Wealth.

Turning to the U.S. economic calendar, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge rose 0.1% in April, as expected.

“Friday's PCE shows that inflation is continuing to soften, which is encouraging news, especially as the economy prepares for higher prices due to tariffs. It will take time for tariffs to work their way through the economy, so we are particularly paying close attention to the inflation readings in 2-3 months from now to better understand how tariffs are affecting consumer prices,” Bellin said.

“Even with a softer PCE reading, it's still above the Fed's 2% target, and we expect the Fed to remain on hold when it comes to interest rates for at least the next six months,” he added.

Also, the U.S. trade deficit narrowed in April. Additionally, Chicago PMI data slid further into contraction territory in May.

data.title: JPMorgan's Dimon discusses dangers from 'tectonic shifts' in geopolitics, the global economy
data.description:
"Tectonic plates are shifting" in geopolitics, such as wars and proxy terrorist activities, and in the global economy, JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon said on Friday. And those shifts bring threats. 

More than any threat from China on the global stage, he worries about "the enemy within... What I really worry about is us," he said during an interview on CNBC. "Can we get our own act together?" 

These shifts in the global economy and the divisions within the U.S. have enormous consequences if they're not addressed.

"If we are not the pre-eminent military and the pre-eminent economy in 40 years, we will not be the reserve currency," he said.

It's important to acknowledge the country's flaws, but we should also "celebrate our virtues -- don't denigrate the great things of this country."

"We've got to fix our permitting, our regulations, our immigration, our taxation, which I think is on their way,"  he said. Other areas that need to be fixed include inner city schools and the health care system. If those are fixed, "we'll grow 3% a year, and all these problems will disappear."

But the U.S. won't get much done if it remains divided. "If you put a team on the field, and they're torn apart, you're going to lose," he said. "That's kind of us right now."

He sees maintaining military alliances as the most important issue. "Spend whatever you have to spend to have the strongest military in the world," Dimon said.

"I'm hoping the goal of the Trump administration is this — keep the Western military alliances together," he added. "If the world can't rely on America's military umbrella, they are going to be looking at alternatives, and there's only one other real alternative out there, or get a nuclear weapon."

Next in importance is maintaining and expanding economic alliances. "The goal should be to help Europe get stronger...Do the trade agreements, the investment agreements that will keep the Western world together. Those two things will keep the world safe and free for democracy."

At this point, the best the U.S. can hope for is to finish 15 important trade deals, and not just with the country's traditional alliances.

I would engage with China," Dimon said. "They're not scared, folks."

The U.S. shouldn't be aiming to bring back the manufacturing of such things as TVs, T-shirts, or sneakers. "It should be the stuff that relates to national security, such as rare earths, pharmaceutical ingredients, maybe AI, chips," or where trade is used to mercilessly dominate an industry. "That might be cars, batteries, solar panels, stuff like that."

He said there's no need to rethink national security. "We know what constitutes national security." That leads Dimon back to military preparedness.

"We shouldn't be stockpiling bitcoin (BTC-USD). We should be stockpiling guns, bullets, tanks, planes, drones, rare earths," he said. "We know what to do, we just now gotta go about doing it."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: TSMC considers constructing advanced chip fab in UAE: report
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) is considering building an advanced chip fabrication facility in the United Arab Emirates, according to Bloomberg.

The company has discussed the possibility with officials in U.S. President Donald Trump's administration, as it would require approval from Washington, D.C., to build the facility, the report said, citing people familiar with the issue. The proposed project would be similar to the facility TSMC is currently building in Arizona. Even if approved, it would take several years before breaking ground.

TSMC makes chips for the biggest hardware companies in the world, such as Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Broadcom (AVGO).

The UAE has been busy securing partnerships with tech companies to increase its role in the artificial intelligence revolution. Earlier this month, the President of the United Arab Emirates, His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, unveiled the first phase of a new 5-gigawatt AI campus in Abu Dhabi. The campus will be built by UAE-based firm G42, which already has a relationship with various U.S. companies, including Nvidia and Microsoft (MSFT), and operated in partnership with several unnamed U.S. companies.

data.title: SA analyst downgrades: PLTR, PSTG, NTLA, ARWR
data.description:
Here are the latest stock rating downgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR). Hold to sell by Bay Area Ideas. "Despite strong fundamentals, Palantir's P/S ratio is extremely elevated, trading at a massive premium to the sector and disconnected from growth rates. Given overvaluation and receding technical strength, I downgrade Palantir to a sell, as risk of a significant down move now outweighs bullish factors."

Pure Storage (NYSE:PSTG). Hold to sell by Gary Alexander. "At current valuations above 30x P/E, Pure Storage is too expensive given its operational challenges—I'm downgrading to sell and recommend locking in gains."

Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA). Buy to hold by Edmund Ingham. "Given the unresolved safety questions and high cash burn, I remain on the sidelines, seeing both high risk and high reward potential for NTLA stock."

Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ARWR). Hold to sell by Edmund Ingham. "Arrowhead lacks a standout candidate for me and is subject to significant competitive threats, leading to my rating downgrade for ARWR stock."

data.title: U.S. rig count slides for fifth straight week in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of total drilling rigs in the U.S. fell for the fifth straight week, down by 3 to 563 to the lowest level since November 2021, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest weekly survey.

The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by 4 to 461 this week, also the lowest since November 2021, but gas rigs gained 1 to 99, while 3 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Baker Hughes said this week's decline dropped the total count by 37 rigs, or 6%, from a year ago at this time.

Total drilling rigs in the Permian Basin, the biggest U.S. oil-producing shale formation, fell by 1 to 278, also the lowest since November 2021.

For August, the total count finished down by 24, its third straight monthly drop and the biggest monthly decline since August 2023.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)
